signals.headline: **IMF forecast (pre-April 2026):** Projects U
signals.raw: **IMF forecast (pre-April 2026):** Projects U.S. core PCE inflation at 2.6% by end-2026 (from 2.9% in 2025), GDP growth at 2.4% in 2026, and fed funds rate easing minimally from 3.6% to 3.4%; attributes persistent inflation and growth drag to tariffs at 7-8.5% effective rate.[1]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: **Powell statements (Dec 2025-Mar 2026):** Described economy as "very unusual" with tariffs driving most inflation overs
signals.raw: **Powell statements (Dec 2025-Mar 2026):** Described economy as "very unusual" with tariffs driving most inflation overshoot (goods peak Q1 2026); noted Fed cut rates defensively amid weakening labor market (official jobs overstated by ~60k/month); monitored energy spikes from Iran war but limited Fed response.[2][3][5]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: **Tariff update (April 2, 2026):** Effective U
signals.raw: **Tariff update (April 2, 2026):** Effective U.S. tariff rate at 11.0% (highest since 1943 excl. 2025), implying 1.0% short-run consumer price rise if Section 122 extended (0.6% if expires); slightly reduces 2026 GDP level but boosts growth rate post-2025 shock.[4]
